grades.label: Market Position
grades.evidence: The filing shows a large and growing membership base (47.6 million paid members in 2016, 90% U.S./Canada renewal rate) and repeated references to competing with major retailers and other warehouse clubs, indicating Costco is a top-tier, category-leading operator though it coexists with other national competitors (e.g., Sam’s Club, BJ’s, Wal‑Mart).

grades.label: Moat Type & Strength
grades.evidence: Management emphasizes "volume purchasing, efficient distribution and reduced handling" that allow operation "profitably at significantly lower gross margins," limited SKUs (~3,700 per warehouse), and a strong private‑label (Kirkland Signature) — all scale-driven advantages that are described as durable and broadly embedded in the business model.

grades.label: Market Quality
grades.evidence: The filing describes a highly competitive but steady retail/wholesale market with predictable membership renewals and ongoing demand (online was ~4% of sales in 2016), suggesting a mature, stable market rather than one in secular decline or rapid disruption.

grades.label: Leadership Quality
grades.evidence: Capital allocation shows discipline and shareholder returns (regular dividends, a $5.00 special dividend in 2015, consistent buybacks and repayment of notes) alongside substantial, planned reinvestment (capital expenditures ~$2.65B in 2016 and guidance ~$2.6–2.8B for 2017). Strategy is coherent and consistent (membership model, low prices, limited SKUs, expansion of warehouses and ancillary services), and execution is evidenced by membership and cardholder growth, high renewal rates, and 29 new warehouses opened in 2016, supporting an overall 4/5 leadership score.
